condensed_reasoning:
## Research

I reviewed a comprehensive forecasting trace for the question: "How many problems solved by AI will the official FrontierMath Open Problems index show on May 1, 2026?"

The agent conducted thorough research using multiple tools:
- Fetched the Metaculus question details (post #43127) establishing the resolution criteria
- Retrieved the Epoch AI FrontierMath Open Problems pages to determine current status
- Searched for news about recent AI solves on the benchmark
- Checked Manifold prediction markets for calibration
- Searched X/Twitter for breaking news
- Used web search and Exa for comprehensive coverage

## Key Evidence

**Current State (as of April 11, 2026):**
- **1 problem solved**: Ramsey Hypergraphs, solved March 23, 2026 by GPT-5.4 Pro (confirmed by problem contributor Will Brian)
- **15 total problems** across 4 tiers: Moderately interesting (4), Solid result (5), Major advance (3), Breakthrough (3)
- **1 problem removed** (March 5, 2026) — not counted as solved; didn't meet publishability bar

**Partial Progress:**
- Small Diophantine problem has **3/9 equations solved** by GPT-5.4 Pro, but Epoch AI explicitly states the remaining 6 "will require a genuinely new mathematical approach" — not on track for full solve

**AI Capability Context:**
- FrontierMath Tiers 1-4 performance improved from ~2% (late 2024) to ~50% (March 2026)
- Multiple frontier models (GPT-5.4 Pro, Opus 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro) can solve the Ramsey problem
- GPT-5.2 Pro and Gemini 3 Deep Think showed "little promise" on the open problems

**Time Constraints:**
- Only ~20 days until May 1 resolution
- No new solves in the 19 days since March 23
- Verification by problem contributors adds friction before website updates

**Market Data:**
- Manifold market for full-year 2026 gives ~15% to just 1 solve, ~15% to 2, ~16% to 3
- This implies most traders expect more than 1 by year-end, but May 1 is much sooner

## Conclusion

The agent computed a Poisson base rate: ~0.1 solves/week × 3 weeks = λ=0.3, giving ~74% probability of no new solve. The final estimate:
- **"1 or fewer": 70%** — Status quo heavily favored
- **"2": 22%** — Requires Small Diophantine completion or another Moderately interesting problem cracked
- **"Greater than 2": 8%** — Would need extraordinary breakthrough or new model release

by_tool.mcp__markets__get_metaculus_questions: 1 call, 0 errors, impact: none, value: high — established resolution criteria and current state
by_tool.mcp__search__fetch_url: 5 calls, 0 errors, impact: none, value: high — successfully extracted problem status from Epoch AI pages
by_tool.mcp__search__web_search: 4 calls, 0 errors, impact: none, value: high — comprehensive coverage of FrontierMath news and current state
by_tool.mcp__markets__manifold_price: 1 call, 0 errors, impact: none, value: medium — provided calibration from full-year market
by_tool.mcp__search__search_exa: 1 call, 0 errors, impact: none, value: medium — supplementary articles on Ramsey solve
by_tool.mcp__asknews__search_news: 1 call, 1 error (403 rate limit), impact: minor, value: wasted — rate limit prevented news search
by_tool.mcp__asknews__search_x_twitter: 1 call, 0 errors, impact: none, value: low — minimal results but confirmed no breaking news
by_tool.mcp__notes__reflection: 1 call, 0 errors, impact: none, value: high — comprehensive factor analysis with base rate calculation

capability_gaps: JavaScript-rendered content extraction — the main FrontierMath Open Problems page likely requires JS to show full problem list
subtle_bugs: fetch_url on epoch.ai/frontiermath/open-problems/ returned incomplete content (possibly JS-rendered) — agent worked around by fetching individual problem pages

classification.approach: Base rate calculation from historical solve rate (Poisson model) combined with status quo analysis and prediction market calibration
classification.key_uncertainty: Whether a new frontier AI model will be released before May 1 that exceeds current capabilities enough to crack additional problems

risk_flags.evidence: Agent assigns only 8% to '>2' but the trace notes AI math capabilities went from 2% to 50% on FrontierMath Tiers 1-4 in ~18 months. Given this pace of improvement and active testing by multiple labs, a new model release could plausibly crack several problems simultaneously. The 8% may underweight tail risk from discontinuous AI progress.

strengths: Used Poisson base rate calculation (λ=0.3 for 3 weeks) to anchor status quo probability at ~74%, providing quantitative rigor
Correctly identified that Small Diophantine's 3/9 progress is not close to a full solve — the explicit Epoch AI statement about 'genuinely new mathematical approach' was key evidence
Cross-checked with Manifold full-year market and appropriately adjusted for the shorter May 1 timeframe
Verified no new solves announced since March 23 via multiple sources (web search, X/Twitter, Exa)
Factor analysis correctly weighted status quo heavily while acknowledging AI capability advancement as countervailing force
weaknesses: Did not attempt to estimate verification delay — how long between AI finding a solution and Epoch AI updating the website? This could affect whether a solution found in late April would be counted
Manifold market interpretation could be more precise — the agent noted '~15% to just 1 solve' but this is P(exactly 1) not P(≤1 by May 1), limiting direct comparability
No explicit search for upcoming AI model release announcements (GPT-5.5, Claude 5) — the new model risk was treated as speculative when scheduled releases might be findable

issues: mcp__asknews__search_news hit 403 rate limit (ForbiddenError: 403012 - Usage limit exceeded)

actionable_improvements: Search for AI company announcements about upcoming model releases to better assess the 'new model' tail risk
Attempt to estimate verification/publication delay from the Ramsey Hypergraphs case (solved March 23 by external users, when was it verified and posted?)
For prediction market calibration, extract implied probability of ≤1 by May 1 from the full-year market structure, not just cite the P(exactly 1 by EOY)
